REPLY TO OPPOSITION AND OBJECTION TO RECEIVERS' MOTION REQUESTING INSTRUCTIONS TO RECEIVER AS TO THE DISTRIBUTION OF PROCEEDS FROM THE SALE OF LIQUOR LICENSES FILED BY PLAINTIFF CONCORDE EQUITY II, LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, RECEIVER KEVIN SINGER'S MOTION FOR ORDER: 1)INSTRUCTING RECEIVER AS TO THE DISTRIBUTION OF PROCEEDS FROM THE SALE OF LIQUOR LICENSE 2)APPROVING AND SETTLING RECEIVER'S FINAL REPORT AND ACCOUNT 3)APPROVING FINAL COMPENSATION AND REIMBURSEMENT OF COSTS FOR RECEIVER 4)EXONERATING ALL BONDS 5)APPROVING AND RATIFYING ALL OF RECEIVER'S ACTS AND TRANSACTIONS 6)RETAINING JURISDICTION REGARDING THIS RECEIVERSHIP - THE COURT GRANTS THE RECEIVER'S MOTION AND MOTION FOR AN ORDER IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE COURT INSTRUCTS THE RECEIVER TO DISTRIBUTE THE PROCEEDS FROM THE SALE OF THE LIQUOR LICENSE AS FOLLOWS: DISTRIBUTION OF FUNDS IN ESCROW TO KEVIN SINGER, SUPERIOR COURT RECEIVER IS THE AMOUNT OF $17,295.55 AND TO PLAINTIFF CONCORDE EQUITY II IN THE AMOUNT OF $58,635.05. THERE IS TO BE NO DISTRIBUTION OF FUNDS IN ESCROW TO CTA, DBI BEVERAGE, MATAGRANO, INC., TONY CARRACI, ROBIN REICHERT AND MUSE, INC. THE COURT APPROVED AND SETTLES THE RECEIVER'S FINAL REPORT AND ACCOUNTING. THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT THE RECEIVER'S ACTIONS WERE CORRECT AND PROPER AND IN THE BEST INTERESTS OF THE RECEIVERSHIP. ALL BONDS POSTED HEREIN ARE EXONERATED. THE COURT RETAINS JURISDICTION OVER ANY MATTERS OR CLAIMS WHICH MAY LATER ARISE CONCERNING THIS RECEIVERSHIP ESTATE. THE RECEIVER IS HEREBY DISCHARGED AND THE RECEIVERSHIP TERMINATED. THIS CASE IS DISMISSED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. IN ADDITION, THE COURT STATED THAT THE ORDER WAS STAYED UNTIL 08/08/11. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: GISELLE CASEY, CSR #8098

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/JUDGMENT CREDITOR CONCORDE EQUITY II, LLC'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION TO RESTRAIN PARTIES FROM DISPOSING OF COLLATERAL AND ASSETS IS DENIED. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS RARELY GRANTED WHERE A SUIT FOR DAMAGES PROVIDES A CLEAR REMEDY. A DAMAGE REMEDY IN THIS CASE IS NOT PRECLUDED BY LAW. WHILE BOTH PLAINTIFF AND INTERVENORS HAVE SUBSTANTIAL CLAIMS, THERE ARE SERIOUS DOUBTS THAT PLAINTIFF CAN DEMONSTRATE HE HAS A REASONABLE PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING. MOREOVER, IN WEIGHING THE RESPECTIVE HARDSHIPS OF THE PARTIES, IT APPEARS THAT INTERVENORS WILL SUFFER GREATER HARM IF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. IN ADDITION, THE COURT STATED THAT THE TRO EXPIRED TODAY (06/10/11). MR. KAUS WILL PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: JOANNE FARRELL, CSR #4838

calendar.matters:
Ntc And Motion For Or Der Tto Instruct Receiver As To Distribution Of Proceeds And Final Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RECEIVER KEVIN SINGER'S MOTION FOR ORDER: 1)INSTRUCTING RECEIVER AS TO THE DISTRIBUTION OF PROCEEDS FROM THE SALE OF LIQUOR LICENSE 2)APPROVING AND SETTLING RECEIVER'S FINAL REPORT AND ACCOUNT 3)APPROVING FINAL COMPENSATION AND REIMBURSEMENT OF COSTS FOR RECEIVER 4)EXONERATING ALL BONDS 5)APPROVING AND RATIFYING ALL OF RECEIVER'S ACTS AND TRANSACTIONS 6)RETAINING JURISDICTION REGARDING THIS RECEIVERSHIP.

APPEARANCES: Stuart A. Katz, Esq. of Law Offices of Stuart A. Katz 949/660-1916 for pltf/judgment creditor (appearing via CourtCall); Kevin Singer, 848-2984 Court Receiver & moving party; Douglas B. Provencher, Esq. of Provencher & Flatt LLP 707/284-2380 for creditor Creditors Trade Association, Inc. & opposing party (appearing via CourtCall); Michael W. Sganga, Esq. of Law Office of Lipton, Piper & Sganga, LLP 362-6286 for creditor Tony Carracci & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - The Court grants the Receiver's Motion and Motion for an Order in its entirety.
The Court instructs the Receiver to distribute the proceeds from the sale of the liquor license as follows:
Distribution of funds in escrow to Kevin Singer, Superior Court Receiver is the amount of $17,295.55 and to Plaintiff Concorde Equity II in the amount of $58,635.05. There is to be no distribution of funds in escrow to CTA, DBI Beverage, Matagrano, Inc., Tony Carraci, Robin Reichert and Muse, Inc.
The Court approved and Settles the Receiver's Final Report and Accounting. The Court further finds that the Receiver's actions were correct and proper and in the best interests of the Receivership.
All bonds posted herein are exonerated.
The Court retains jurisdiction over any matters or claims which may later arise concerning this Receivership estate.
The Receiver is hereby discharged and the Receivership terminated.
This case is dismissed without prejudice. Order signed in open court. In addition, the Court stated that the order was stayed until 08/08/11. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098

Notice And Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Restrain Parties From Disposing Of Collateral And Assets
Off Calendar Jun-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF/JUDGMENT CREDITOR CONCORDE EQUITY II, LLC'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION TO RESTRAIN PARTIES FROM DISPOSING OF COLLATERAL AND ASSETS.

APPEARANCES: Stuart A. Katz, Esq. of Law Offices of Stuart A. Katz 949/660-1916 for pltf & moving party (appearing via CourtCall); Stephen Kaus, Esq. & Scott M. McLeod, Esq. of Cooper White & Cooper 765-0378 for Intervenors & opposing parties George Gamble and Nancy Gamble; Christopher I. Ritter, Esq. & Gary S. Raskin, Esq. of Raskin Ritter LLP 310/202-5544 for Intervenor & opposing party Matthew Schwarz (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction is denied. Injunctive Relief is rarely granted where a suit for damages provides a clear remedy. A damage remedy in this case is not precluded by law. While both Plaintiff and Intervenors have substantial claims, there are serious doubts that Plaintiff can demonstrate he has a reasonable probability of prevailing. Moreover, in weighing the respective hardships of the parties, it appears that Intervenors will suffer greater harm if the Preliminary Injunction is granted. In addition, the Court stated that the TRO expired today (06/10/11). Mr. Kaus will prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Joanne Farrell, CSR #4838
